---
title: "What is a 'good' forecast?"
description: "A project sharing talk at the University of Exeter on what makes forecasts useful for decision-making."
author: "Prof. Bahman Rostami-Tabar and Harsha Halgamuwe Hewage"
date: "2026-06-10"
image: "cover.png"
categories:
  - Forecasting
  - Decision Support
  - Uncertainty
  - Social Good
link-external-newwindow: true
---


```{=html}
<!-- Add Font Awesome for icons -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css">


<div class="hero-section">
  <h1 class="title">What is a 'good' forecast?</h1>
  <p class="description">A project sharing talk at the University of Exeter on what makes forecasts useful for decision-making.</p>
  <p class="author-date">Prof. Bahman Rostami-Tabar and Harsha Halgamuwe Hewage | 2026-06-10</p>
  <div class="cta-buttons">
    <a href="slides/exeter_visit.html" target="_blank" class="btn-custom btn-primary">
      <i class="fas fa-bookmark"></i> Slides
    </a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="content-section">
  <p>This talk was prepared for a visit to the University of Exeter to share ongoing project work on what makes a forecast useful in practice. The presentation frames forecasting as more than accuracy, connecting model performance with decision-making, uncertainty, implementation, and social-good outcomes.</p>
</div>
```
